completed: Verified the live public health surface: /, /search, /methodology, /sources, /membership, /analytics, /api/analytics/snapshot, and anonymous /api/search all returned successfully on April 7, 2026.

Confirmed a live auth boundary defect: logout deleted session rows, but authenticateToken only trusted JWT verification plus the users table, so revoked tokens could still unlock protected content until expiry.
Patched server-side auth to require an unexpired matching session row for every bearer token, closing logout/session revocation gaps across chapter unlocks, downloads, and user APIs.
Patched ChapterPage and ReadTheBookPage to re-request scope-appropriate chapter payloads on auth transitions and clear stale full-content state when the reader auth scope changes.
Verified the fix locally in the active workspace and in a clean detached worktree with npx tsc --noEmit and npm run build.

Confirmed the deployed Railway build revoked logged-out bearer tokens on April 7, 2026: the same token now gets 401 from /api/auth/me and /api/downloads/the-record.pdf after logout while public routes still return 200.
Added a repo-native auth smoke runner at scripts/verify-auth-flows.mjs plus npm run verify:auth, then executed it successfully against https://veritasworldwide.com.
Normalized public evidence taxonomy copy so the live trust layer uses Verified / Circumstantial / Disputed consistently across /deep-state, /methodology, and the chapter-20 reader data path.
Repaired MethodologyPage in-page navigation by adding real hash targets for the sidebar sections, then rebuilt generated chapter data and prerender output with npx tsc --noEmit and npm run build.

Added a repo-native source-link checker at scripts/verify-source-links.mjs plus npm run verify:sources, with report output written to .claude-state/source-link-report.md and .claude-state/source-link-report.json.
Removed the unsupported expandedArticlesB news batch from the live article corpus because its source URLs were not publication-grade and should not have remained publicly searchable.
Repaired deterministic citation truncations across the chapter/news corpus and reran the verifier, reducing actionable source-link issues from 64 to 39 while preserving the public trust routes.

Replaced the monolithic legacy src/data/chapters.ts manuscript copy with a module-backed bootstrap/search entrypoint sourced from src/data/chapters/*.ts, removing a second chapter-content code path that could drift away from the runtime and generated corpus.
Corrected the remaining Chinese evidence label from the old court-certified wording to the canonical Verified meaning, then revalidated the repo with npx tsc --noEmit and npm run build.

Refined the publication shell in src/App.tsx by collapsing the header into primary versus trust utilities, grouping the mobile drawer, simplifying shell buttons, and reducing footer link sprawl while preserving the live route surface, then revalidated with npx tsc --noEmit and npm run build.

Built a new Veritas Institute vertical with a separate shell, distinct visual system, 100-topic search-intent catalog, course pages, guide pages, methodology page, and Book of Knowledge route backed by src/data/instituteCatalog.ts.
Added a browser-side Book of Knowledge PDF generator for the institute using jspdf so the catalog can export into a printable field manual instead of staying web-only.

Extended scripts/prerender.mjs and the sitemap pipeline so /institute, /institute/book, /institute/methodology, and every institute course/guide route are prerendered or listed for crawlability, then revalidated with npx tsc --noEmit and npm run build.
next_task: Wait for Railway to serve the institute build, then verify /institute, /institute/book, and representative /institute/courses/:slug plus /institute/guides/:slug routes at 390px and 430px before deciding whether to deepen the catalog bodies or harden the PDF export further.
next_priorities: Verify the deployed institute shell on /institute, /institute/book, and representative course/guide routes at 390px and 430px in a real browser session, including navigation, scanability, and PDF CTA clarity.
Pressure-test the institute PDF export on a realistic browser/device mix and decide whether to move the Book of Knowledge export to a build-time or server-side artifact for very large manuals.
Decide whether the institute deserves a stronger editorial entry point on the homepage or in a dedicated front-page module beyond the new shell/footer discoverability.
Verify the deployed publication shell on /, /read, /news, and /membership at 390px and 430px in a real browser session, including the grouped mobile drawer and sticky primary navigation.
Verify the /chapter/:id and /read auth upgrade/downgrade experience at 390px and 430px in a real browser session.
Replace or retire the remaining broken source links concentrated in src/data/israelDossierExpanded.ts and src/data/profileData.ts, preferring official records or archive-backed citations.
Add Sentry or equivalent production monitoring to capture auth/API failures before readers report them.
Decide whether to wire npm run verify:auth and npm run verify:sources into CI or a scheduled operational check against Railway.
